record_of_employment.name_and_address_of_company_and_type_of_:
Gulf Coast Community Bank
1201 Main Street, Suite 900
Houston, TX 77002
Regional community banking and financial services
record_of_employment.reason_for_leaving_employment_1: Seeking a growth opportunity with a minority-owned institution that has a strong focus on community development lending and small business support. After five and a half years in my current role, promotion opportunities have plateaued due to a very flat branch structure. I am looking for a role where I can take on more responsibility in credit analysis and portfolio management, work more closely with underserved neighborhoods, and continue my professional development through formal training programs.
record_of_employment.title_job_duties_employment_1:
Senior Personal Banker / Relationship Consultant

• Managed a portfolio of approximately 350 retail and small-business clients, serving as their primary point of contact for deposit accounts, consumer loans, small business lines of credit, and online banking services.
• Opened and maintained checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, and money market accounts, ensuring all KYC (Know Your Customer) and CIP (Customer Identification Program) requirements were satisfied, and verified identification and documentation for compliance.
• Conducted detailed needs assessments with walk-in and existing customers to recommend appropriate banking products, including overdraft protection, credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, and HELOCs, with a strong emphasis on suitability and responsible borrowing.
• Prepared and submitted loan applications, gathered income documentation (pay stubs, tax returns, bank statements), and worked closely with the centralized underwriting team. I regularly reviewed credit reports, explained adverse action notices to clients, and helped them understand how to improve their credit over time.
• Assisted small business clients with account setup (DBA, LLC, and nonprofit accounts), ACH origination, remote deposit capture, merchant services referrals, and basic cash management solutions.
• Handled complex customer service issues, including disputed transactions, fraud claims, and account compromises. Coordinated with the fraud department to place appropriate holds, close and re-open accounts, and assist customers in filing affidavits and police reports when necessary.
• Balanced the branch vault and teller cash recyclers on a rotating basis, verified cash shipments, and followed dual-control procedures for cash audits, safe deposit box access, and night drop processing.
• Trained and mentored three newly hired personal bankers and several tellers on branch procedures, compliance requirements (including BSA/AML red flags and OFAC checks), and system navigation. Frequently served as the informal “go-to” person for questions on the core banking system.
• Consistently exceeded quarterly sales and service goals, particularly in checking account growth and digital adoption (online banking, mobile deposit, and e-statements), while maintaining high customer satisfaction scores based on post-interaction surveys.
• Participated in community outreach events, such as financial literacy workshops at local high schools and small business centers, where I presented on topics including budgeting, building credit, and avoiding predatory lending.
• Ensured adherence to internal controls and regulatory requirements, including Reg E, Reg CC, BSA/AML, and privacy rules, by carefully documenting customer interactions and maintaining accurate records in the CRM and core system.

8450 Westheimer Road
Houston, TX 77063
Member-owned credit union providing consumer banking and lending services
record_of_employment.reason_for_leaving_employment_2: Offered an opportunity at Gulf Coast Community Bank with broader responsibilities, higher transaction volumes, and a more structured training program in lending and relationship management. I also wanted to gain experience in a larger, multi-branch environment with more complex products and systems.
record_of_employment.title_job_duties_employment_2:
Member Services Representative / Loan Officer Trainee

• Served as a front-line representative assisting members with day-to-day transactions, including deposits, withdrawals, transfers, loan payments, and cashier’s checks, while maintaining accuracy and balancing my cash drawer daily.
• Opened new member accounts (checking, savings, youth accounts, and share certificates) and explained account terms, fees, and digital banking features in clear, accessible language.
• Completed intake for consumer loan applications (auto, personal, and credit card), gathered necessary documentation, and entered information into the loan origination system for underwriting review.
• Conducted initial credit report reviews, discussed basic credit factors (payment history, utilization, and inquiries) with members, and provided guidance on steps to improve their creditworthiness.
• Assisted with delinquency follow-up by making reminder calls, sending letters, and working with members to set up payment arrangements or modify due dates when appropriate, while adhering to internal policies and fair debt collection standards.
• Educated members on the credit union difference, including membership eligibility, not-for-profit structure, and how profits are returned through better rates and lower fees.
• Cross-sold ancillary products such as GAP insurance and extended warranties on auto loans by clearly explaining coverage, costs, and when these products might or might not make sense for a member.
• Participated in Saturday rotation and handled a high volume of transactions while maintaining a focus on service quality and compliance with cash-handling procedures.
• Assisted the branch manager with basic reporting tasks, such as daily new account summaries and branch sales tracking spreadsheets.

Houston, TX 77098
Retail electronics store
record_of_employment.reason_for_leaving_employment_3: Transitioned from retail sales into financial services to pursue a more stable, long-term career path with regular hours, benefits, and opportunities for advancement. I was also interested in leveraging my customer service skills in a setting where I could help people with financial decision-making rather than purely retail purchases.
record_of_employment.title_job_duties_employment_3:
Sales Associate / Shift Lead

• Assisted customers in selecting computers, tablets, phones, and home electronics by asking detailed questions about their needs, budgets, and technical comfort level.
• Maintained up-to-date knowledge of product specifications, warranties, and promotional financing offers, and explained these clearly to customers to help them make informed decisions.
• Processed sales transactions, returns, and exchanges using the POS system, handled cash and credit card payments, and balanced the cash drawer at the end of shift.
• As a shift lead, supervised 3–5 team members on evening and weekend shifts, assigned tasks (restocking, merchandising, cleaning), and ensured that closing procedures were completed accurately.
• Resolved customer complaints related to defective merchandise, warranty claims, and billing discrepancies, often coordinating with the store manager or vendor representatives.
• Assisted with inventory counts, receiving shipments, and setting up promotional displays for new product launches.
• Regularly exceeded monthly sales targets and was recognized twice as “Associate of the Month” for high customer satisfaction scores and strong add-on sales performance (accessories, protection plans, and software).

authorization_and_restrictions.employers_you_do_not_wish_us_to_contact: Please do not contact Bayou City Electronics (Employment 3) at this time, as I do not want my current job search to be widely known in that older network. You may contact Gulf Coast Community Bank and Lone Star Credit Union.

skills_and_qualifications.skills_and_aptitudes_that_would_qualify_:
Over the past decade, I have built a strong foundation in retail and consumer banking, with particular strengths in customer service, relationship management, and day-to-day branch operations. I believe these experiences align closely with the expectations for a role at Unity National Bank, especially given your focus on serving diverse communities and expanding access to responsible financial services.

BANKING AND CUSTOMER SERVICE EXPERIENCE
• Front-line banking expertise: In my current role as a Senior Personal Banker at Gulf Coast Community Bank, I handle a wide range of customer needs—from opening basic checking accounts for first-time bankers to assisting small business owners with more complex account structures and cash management tools. I am comfortable working in a fast-paced branch environment where I move between teller transactions, customer consultations, and back-office follow-up work throughout the day.
• Relationship-building and retention: I manage a book of approximately 350 clients and have learned how to maintain ongoing relationships rather than focusing solely on one-time transactions. For example, I have worked with several families over multiple years, starting by helping their teenagers open student checking accounts, later guiding them through their first auto loans, and eventually referring them to our mortgage department when they were ready to buy homes. I try to remember personal details (children’s names, small businesses they own, important dates) so interactions feel genuine and not transactional.
• Handling difficult conversations: I frequently handle delicate issues such as overdrafts, declined transactions, and fraud claims. I’ve found that listening carefully, acknowledging the customer’s frustration, and explaining bank policies in plain, non-technical language helps de-escalate tension. For instance, when a long-time client had multiple overdrafts due to a sudden reduction in work hours, I walked her through her transaction history line by line, helped her set up low-balance alerts, and showed her how to organize automatic payments to avoid layering fees.

LENDING, CREDIT, AND COMPLIANCE AWARENESS
• Foundational lending knowledge: While I am not an underwriter, I regularly collect documentation, review credit reports, and prepare loan files for consumer loans and small business lines of credit. I understand the basic factors that underwriters consider (DTI, credit score, collateral, stability of income) and can explain to customers why certain decisions are made.
• Credit education: Many of our customers have limited credit history or have made mistakes in the past. I often spend extra time walking them through how credit scores are calculated, why on-time payments matter, and how to responsibly use credit cards and installment loans to rebuild their credit. I see this as a core part of community banking—helping people understand the system rather than just saying “approved” or “denied.”
• Regulatory and compliance orientation: I am familiar with day-to-day compliance touchpoints in a branch, including KYC/CIP requirements, BSA/AML red flags (unusual cash activity, structuring, inconsistent information), and privacy rules. I complete required annual training on topics such as the Bank Secrecy Act, OFAC, Reg E, and Reg CC, and I’m comfortable following procedures even when they slow down a transaction, because I understand the risk to the institution and the community if we cut corners.

TECHNICAL AND SYSTEMS SKILLS
• Core banking systems: I have worked with two different core banking platforms and can adapt quickly to new systems. I am comfortable navigating multiple screens, entering and verifying data, and documenting notes in CRM systems so that other bankers have context when they interact with the same customer later.
• Digital banking support: I regularly help customers enroll in and troubleshoot online and mobile banking—resetting passwords, setting up mobile deposit, enrolling in e-statements, and explaining how to use alerts and budgeting tools. This has been especially important for older customers or those who are not as comfortable with technology; I often walk them through the mobile app step-by-step and encourage them to come back if they need more help.
• Microsoft Office and basic reporting: I use Excel for simple tracking (e.g., personal sales results, follow-up call lists), Word for drafting customer letters, and Outlook for managing my calendar and follow-up reminders. I am not an advanced analyst, but I am comfortable with spreadsheets, basic formulas, and keeping information organized.

SOFT SKILLS AND PERSONAL QUALITIES
• Communication and empathy: I grew up in a working-class neighborhood in Houston, and many of my family members either did not have bank accounts or had negative experiences with financial institutions. This background makes me very conscious of how intimidating banking can feel. I make a deliberate effort to use plain language, avoid jargon, and check for understanding—especially when explaining fees, interest rates, or loan terms.
• Bilingual communication: I am fluent in English and conversational in Spanish. In my current branch, I frequently assist Spanish-speaking customers with basic transactions, account opening, and simple explanations of products. For more complex conversations (such as detailed loan terms), I work alongside a fully bilingual colleague, but my Spanish skills often serve as an important first bridge so customers feel more comfortable.
• Attention to detail: Because small mistakes in banking can have big consequences, I am meticulous about double-checking account numbers, names, and identification documents. I am also careful about documenting conversations and follow-up commitments so customers are not left feeling ignored or misled.
• Team orientation: I enjoy working in a branch environment where we rely on each other. At Gulf Coast Community Bank, I often help newer tellers with difficult transactions, balance issues, or customer questions. I also step in to cover the teller line when we are short-staffed, even if I had planned to spend that time on outbound calls or paperwork.

MOTIVATION TO WORK AT UNITY NATIONAL BANK
I am especially drawn to Unity National Bank because of your history as a minority-owned institution and your emphasis on serving communities that have traditionally been underserved by mainstream banks. Many of the customers I work with today struggle with access to affordable credit, financial education, and trust in financial institutions. I want to be part of an organization that sees these challenges not just as risk factors, but as opportunities to build long-term relationships and help customers move toward financial stability.

In a role with Unity National Bank, I would bring:
• A strong, hands-on understanding of what happens in a branch on a daily basis.
• A track record of patient, empathetic customer service with individuals and small businesses.
• Practical experience with consumer lending workflows and credit education.
• A genuine commitment to financial inclusion and community-focused banking.

Overall, my skills, experience, and personal values are aligned with the kind of work Unity National Bank is known for, and I am eager to continue growing professionally in an environment where community impact is a central part of the mission.
